options.label: A
options.text: A magnet made from a coil of wire with electric current flowing through it

options.label: B
options.text: A permanent magnet that produces electricity when moved

options.label: C
options.text: A type of metal that is naturally magnetic and cannot be switched off

options.label: D
options.text: A device that converts magnetism into heat energy

explanation: An electromagnet is created when electric current flows through a wire coil, producing a magnetic field. Unlike permanent magnets, electromagnets can be switched on and off by controlling the current.
